basics.description:
## 🛡️ SpyCloud Sentinel Supreme

![SpyCloud](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/main/docs/images/SpyCloud-Logo-white.png)

**Transform recaptured darknet data into automated identity threat protection.**

This wizard deploys the complete SpyCloud integration including:
- CCF Data Connector with 5 REST API pollers
- 6 custom log tables (118+ columns)
- 5 Logic App playbooks (MDE, CA, Credential, Blocklist, TI Enrichment)
- 43+ analytics rules + Fusion + 5 Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules
- UEBA, Anomaly Detection, EyesOn audit settings
- 3 automation rules (auto-triage, device tasks, auto-close)
- 15 hunting queries across all MITRE ATT&CK tactics
- 4 watchlists (VIP, IOC Blocklist, Approved Domains, High-Value Assets)
- Security Copilot plugin + interactive agent (58+ skills)
- Comprehensive connector + feature recommendations

**Prerequisite:** SpyCloud Enterprise API key from [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com)

[Documentation](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL) · [Architecture](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/blob/main/docs/architecture.md)

options.text: This wizard guides you through every configuration option. Required fields are marked with *. All playbooks and analytics rules are optional and can be toggled individually.

In Azure, the Log Analytics workspace and Sentinel workspace are the SAME resource. Microsoft Sentinel is a solution installed ON TOP of a Log Analytics workspace. When you select or create a workspace here, ALL SpyCloud resources (DCE, DCR, tables, connector, playbooks, rules, workbook) will be deployed into that workspace. If creating new, Sentinel will be automatically enabled.

elements.name: createNewWorkspace
elements.label: Workspace Mode
elements.toolTip: Create a new Log Analytics workspace with Microsoft Sentinel enabled, or use an existing workspace that already has Sentinel.

allowedValues.label: Use existing workspace (Sentinel must be enabled)

elements.name: existingWorkspaceSelector
elements.label: Select Existing Workspace
elements.toolTip: Select an existing Log Analytics workspace that has Microsoft Sentinel enabled.

elements.name: newWorkspaceName
elements.label: New Workspace Name
elements.toolTip: Name for the new workspace. Must be 3-63 characters: letters, numbers, hyphens. Starts with letter.

constraints.validationMessage: 3-63 chars: letters, numbers, hyphens. Must start with a letter.

elements.name: retentionInDays
elements.label: Data Retention (days)
elements.toolTip: How long to retain data in Log Analytics. 90 days is included free. Longer retention incurs additional cost.

options.text: Each playbook is a Logic App with a system-assigned managed identity. API permissions (MDE Machine.Isolate, Graph User.ReadWrite.All) are assigned by the automated post-deploy script or can be granted manually in Azure Portal → Enterprise Applications.

elements.name: enableMdePlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 1: MDE Device Isolation (incident-triggered)
elements.toolTip: Searches MDE for infected devices, isolates them, tags with SpyCloud identifier, logs to audit table. Requires: MDE license + Machine.Isolate permission.

elements.name: mdeIsolationType
elements.label: MDE Isolation Type
elements.toolTip: Full: Completely disconnects device from network (except MDE agent). Selective: Limits connections but allows some traffic.

elements.name: mdeTagName
elements.label: MDE Device Tag
elements.toolTip: Tag applied to isolated devices in MDE for tracking and filtering.

elements.name: enableCaPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 2: Conditional Access Identity Protection (incident-triggered)
elements.toolTip: Resets passwords, revokes sessions, adds users to CA exclusion group. Requires: Entra ID P1+ and User.ReadWrite.All.

elements.name: caSecurityGroupId
elements.label: CA Security Group Object ID (optional)
elements.toolTip: Entra ID security group Object ID for the Conditional Access exclusion group. Compromised users are added to this group to trigger CA policies (e.g., block sign-in, require MFA). Leave blank to skip group assignment.

elements.name: enableCredResponsePlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 3: Credential Response + Teams Alert (incident-triggered)
elements.toolTip: Checks recent sign-ins, forces password reset, revokes sessions, sends real-time Teams MessageCard to SOC. Requires: Security Administrator + Graph IdentityRisk.ReadWrite.All.

elements.name: enableMdeBlocklistPlaybook
elements.label: Playbook 4: MDE Blocklist — Scheduled Severity 25 Scan
elements.toolTip: Runs on a schedule to scan severity 25 (CRITICAL) infostealer records against MDE device inventory. Auto-isolates matched devices. Requires: MDE with API enabled.

elements.name: mdeBlocklistScheduleHours
elements.label: MDE Blocklist Scan Frequency
elements.toolTip: How often Playbook 4 scans for new severity 25 matches.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRule
elements.label: Create primary infostealer detection rule
elements.toolTip: Creates the primary analytics rule that detects severity 20+ exposures and generates Sentinel incidents.

elements.name: enableAutomationRule
elements.label: Create automation rule (auto-trigger playbooks on incidents)
elements.toolTip: Creates an automation rule that fires the enabled playbooks when SpyCloud incidents are created.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRulesLibrary
elements.label: Deploy FULL analytics rules library (22 rules, ALL disabled by default)
elements.toolTip: Deploys all 22 SpyCloud detection rules: plaintext passwords, PII exposure, session cookies, device re-infection, multi-domain exposure, geographic anomaly, executive/VIP, password reuse, remediation gaps, IdP correlation (Okta/Duo/Ping/Entra), and more. Every rule deploys DISABLED — you review and enable individually.

elements.name: enablePostDeployScript
elements.label: Run automated post-deployment script (resolves DCE/DCR + assigns RBAC)
elements.toolTip: Runs a deployment script inside the ARM deployment that waits for content template resources, resolves DCE Logs Ingestion URI and DCR Immutable ID, and assigns Monitoring Metrics Publisher to all Logic Apps. Eliminates manual post-deploy steps for core setup.

options.text: The SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard is a Sentinel Workbook with real-time charts and tables. It deploys directly into your Sentinel workspace and is accessible from Sentinel → Workbooks.

elements.name: enableWorkbook
elements.label: Deploy SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard (Sentinel Workbook)
elements.toolTip:
Deploys a comprehensive dashboard workbook with 10 visualizations:

• Exposure Overview tiles (total, unique users, devices, plaintext passwords)
• Exposure Trend over time by severity (timechart)
• Severity Distribution (pie chart)
• Top 20 Most Exposed Users (sortable table with severity heatmap)
• Password Type Distribution (bar chart)
• Top Targeted Domains (bar chart)
• Top 20 Infected Devices (table)
• Infections by Country (bar chart)
• Remediation Dashboard (MDE + CA action counts, remediation rate)
• Breach Catalog entries (table)

options.text: Tags are applied to ALL deployed resources. Default tags (solution, version, deployedBy) are always included. Add custom tags below for cost tracking, ownership, or compliance.

elements.name: tagEnvironment
elements.label: Environment Tag
elements.toolTip: Environment classification for all deployed resources.

elements.name: tagOwner
elements.label: Owner Tag
elements.toolTip: Team or individual responsible for these resources.

elements.name: tagCostCenter
elements.label: Cost Center Tag
elements.toolTip: Cost center for billing allocation.

elements.name: teamsChannelWebhook
elements.label: Microsoft Teams Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Incoming webhook for your SOC Teams channel. Used by the Credential Response playbook for real-time alerts. Teams → Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook → Configure.

elements.name: serviceNowInstance
elements.label: ServiceNow Instance URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: ServiceNow instance for automatic ticket creation. Leave blank if not using ServiceNow.

elements.name: slackWebhookUrl
elements.label: Slack Incoming Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Incoming Webhook URL for Slack SOC channel notifications. The Credential Response playbook sends alerts here. Create at: api.slack.com/apps → Incoming Webhooks.

elements.name: jiraWebhookUrl
elements.label: Jira Automation Webhook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Creates Jira issues for credential exposures via Automation webhook.

elements.name: azureDevOpsWebhookUrl
elements.label: Azure DevOps Service Hook URL (optional)
elements.toolTip: Creates work items in Azure DevOps via Service Hook.

elements.name: enableNotifications
elements.label: Enable email/Teams alert notifications
elements.toolTip: Creates an Action Group and health alert that notifies when SpyCloud data ingestion stops for 3+ hours.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Notification Email
elements.toolTip: Email for alert notifications.

constraints.validationMessage: Enter a valid email or leave blank.

elements.name: teamsWebhookUrl
elements.label: Teams Webhook for Health Alerts (optional)
elements.toolTip: Teams webhook for the health monitoring Action Group. Different from the SOC webhook above — this is for infrastructure alerts.

options.text: Slack webhook (configured in SOC Integrations above) also receives health alerts when the connector stops ingesting data.

These rules detect compromised SpyCloud credentials in your identity provider sign-in logs. Requires the IdP connector installed from Sentinel Content Hub.

• Okta: Content Hub → 'Okta Single Sign-On'
• Duo: Content Hub → 'Cisco Duo Security'
• Ping: Syslog/API via AMA agent
• Entra ID: Diagnostic Settings → SignInLogs

elements.name: enableSessionCookieDetection
elements.label: Enable session cookie / token theft detection
elements.toolTip: Rules for severity 25 exposures — stolen browser cookies and session tokens that allow MFA bypass.

elements.name: enableIdentityProviderAlerts
elements.label: Enable identity provider correlation rules (Okta/Duo/Ping/Entra)
elements.toolTip: Deploys 4 rules that cross-reference SpyCloud exposure data with IdP sign-in logs.

options.text: These settings enable core Sentinel platform features that significantly enhance SpyCloud threat detection. UEBA builds behavioral profiles of users, Anomalies uses ML to detect unusual patterns, and Fusion correlates alerts across Microsoft security products into multistage attack incidents.

elements.name: enableUEBA
elements.label: Enable UEBA (User and Entity Behavior Analytics)
elements.toolTip: Builds behavioral profiles for users and entities using Entra ID, SigninLogs, AuditLogs, and AzureActivity. Required for SpyCloud rule #29 (UEBA Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User). Also enables EyesOn audit tracking.

elements.name: enableAnomalies
elements.label: Enable Anomaly Detection (ML-powered)
elements.toolTip: Enables built-in ML anomaly rules that detect unusual sign-in patterns, resource access anomalies, and behavioral deviations. Complements SpyCloud credential exposure alerts.

elements.name: enableFusionRule
elements.label: Enable Fusion Advanced Multistage Attack Detection
elements.toolTip: Patented ML that correlates low-fidelity alerts from multiple Microsoft security products into high-confidence multistage attack incidents. Includes SpyCloud scheduled rules as alert sources.

elements.name: enableMicrosoftSecurityIncidentRules
elements.label: Create Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules
elements.toolTip: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from: Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, Microsoft Cloud App Security, and Defender for Identity. Ensures all Microsoft security alerts flow into Sentinel for cross-correlation with SpyCloud data.

options.text: The TI Enrichment playbook automatically looks up IP addresses from SpyCloud incidents against VirusTotal and AbuseIPDB, adding reputation data as incident comments. Requires a free VirusTotal API key.

elements.name: enableTiEnrichmentPlaybook
elements.label: Deploy TI Enrichment Playbook (VirusTotal + AbuseIPDB)
elements.toolTip: Automatically enriches SpyCloud incidents with external threat intelligence. VirusTotal free tier: 4 req/min.

elements.name: virusTotalApiKey
elements.toolTip: Get your free API key at: https://www.virustotal.com/gui/my-apikey

options.text:
IMPORTANT: The following data connectors and features are NOT deployed by this template but are STRONGLY RECOMMENDED for maximum SpyCloud detection coverage. Enable them manually after deployment.

--- TIER 1: CRITICAL (Required for core SpyCloud rules) ---

1. Microsoft Entra ID (SignInLogs + AuditLogs)
   WHERE: Entra ID > Diagnostic Settings > Send to Log Analytics workspace
   WHY: Rules #6, #7, #10, #18 correlate SpyCloud exposures with sign-in activity
   TABLES: SigninLogs, AuditLogs

2. Microsoft Defender XDR
   WHERE: Sentinel > Content Hub > 'Microsoft Defender XDR' > Install + Connect
   WHY: Rules #11, #12, #13 cross-reference SpyCloud with MDE alerts/devices
   TABLES: DeviceInfo, DeviceNetworkEvents, AlertEvidence, IdentityLogonEvents

3. Azure Activity
   WHERE: Sentinel > Data Connectors > 'Azure Activity' > Connect subscriptions
   WHY: Rule #2 detects exposed users modifying cloud resources; UEBA data source
   TABLES: AzureActivity

4. Office 365
   WHERE: Sentinel > Data Connectors > 'Office 365' > Enable Exchange, SharePoint, Teams
   WHY: Rule #14 detects compromised user mail forwarding; hunting query for BEC
   TABLES: OfficeActivity

--- TIER 2: HIGH VALUE (Enables advanced correlation) ---

5. Microsoft Defender for Cloud
   WHERE: Defender for Cloud > Environment Settings > Enable plans > Continuous Export to LAW
   WHY: MSIC rule auto-creates incidents; Fusion correlates with SpyCloud
   TABLES: SecurityAlert

6. Microsoft Cloud App Security (Defender for Cloud Apps)
   WHERE: Sentinel > Content Hub > 'Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps' > Install
   WHY: Detects impossible travel, OAuth abuse, mass downloads by compromised users
   TABLES: CloudAppEvents

7. Microsoft Defender for Identity
   WHERE: Sentinel > Content Hub > 'Microsoft Defender for Identity' > Install
   WHY: Detects pass-the-hash, golden ticket, DCSync from stolen credentials
   TABLES: IdentityLogonEvents, IdentityDirectoryEvents

8. DNS Analytics
   WHERE: Sentinel > Content Hub > 'DNS' > Install; or DNS server > Diagnostic Settings
   WHY: Rule #X hunts infected IPs resolving malicious domains
   TABLES: DnsEvents

--- TIER 3: RECOMMENDED (Third-party & extended coverage) ---

9. Okta Single Sign-On
   WHERE: Content Hub > 'Okta Single Sign-On' > Install > Configure API token
   WHY: Rule #15 correlates SpyCloud credentials with Okta sign-ins
   TABLES: Okta_CL

10. Cisco Duo Security
    WHERE: Content Hub > 'Cisco Duo Security' > Install > Configure integration key
    WHY: Rule #4 detects compromised credentials in Duo authentications
    TABLES: DuoSecurityAuthentication_CL

11. Palo Alto Networks
    WHERE: Content Hub > 'Palo Alto Networks' > Install > Configure syslog via AMA
    WHY: Firewall correlation for infected IPs; Fusion raw log source
    TABLES: CommonSecurityLog

12. CrowdStrike Falcon
    WHERE: Content Hub > 'CrowdStrike Falcon Endpoint Protection' > Install
    WHY: Alternative EDR correlation for device infections
    TABLES: CommonSecurityLog

13. AWS CloudTrail / GCP Audit Logs
    WHERE: Content Hub > 'Amazon Web Services' or 'Google Cloud Platform' > Install
    WHY: Multi-cloud visibility for compromised users accessing cloud resources
    TABLES: AWSCloudTrail, GCPAuditLogs

14. Threat Intelligence - TAXII
    WHERE: Sentinel > Data Connectors > 'Threat Intelligence - TAXII' > Configure
    WHY: Rule #17 matches SpyCloud IPs against TI feeds; enriches Fusion detection
    TABLES: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator

15. Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence
    WHERE: Sentinel > Content Hub > 'Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence' > Install
    WHY: Microsoft's own TI feed for known malicious infrastructure correlation
    TABLES: ThreatIntelligenceIndicator

--- FEATURES TO ENABLE MANUALLY ---

16. Security Copilot Integration
    WHERE: Upload copilot/SpyCloud_Plugin.yaml and copilot/SpyCloud_Agent.yaml
    HOW: Security Copilot > Settings > Custom Plugins > Upload YAML
    WHY: 58+ investigation skills for autonomous breach analysis

17. Sentinel Repositories (CI/CD)
    WHERE: Sentinel > Settings > Repositories > Connect GitHub/Azure DevOps
    WHY: Version control for analytics rules, playbooks, and hunting queries

--- PERMISSION VALIDATION ---

Run this in Azure Cloud Shell to verify you have the required roles:
  az role assignment list --assignee $(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv) --query '[].roleDefinitionName' -o tsv

Required roles:
  - Microsoft Sentinel Contributor (or higher)
  - Logic App Contributor (for playbooks)
  - Managed Identity Operator (for playbook MSI)
  - Security Admin (for UEBA/EntityAnalytics)
  - Log Analytics Contributor (for tables/DCR)
